commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Apply lazy loading using a new example and justify the result.
Explain code splitting using a new example and justify the result.
Explain bundle size using a new example and justify the result.
Explain avoiding premature optimization using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Lazy loading** → **Code splitting** → **Bundle size** → **Avoiding premature optimization**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Lazy loading is best understood as follows: Lazy loading defers code or resources until a feature is needed, reducing initial work while adding loading and error states.
choices.label: Lazy loading defers code or resources until a feature is needed, reducing initial work while adding loading and error states.

choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
choices.label: First identify the requirement, then apply the relevant rule from Lazy loading, inspect the result, and only then refactor or optimize.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization.
choices.label: Start with a framework abstraction and avoid checking the underlying language behavior.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization.
choices.label: Optimize first, then decide what the code is supposed to do.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization.
choices.label: Copy an unrelated implementation, hide the assumptions, and test only the happy path.

questions.explanation: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.

choices.label: Avoid Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization completely because all abstractions reduce maintainability.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization.
choices.label: Choose based only on line count, without considering readability, correctness, or future change.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization.
choices.label: Use Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization in every file because more abstraction is always better.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
choices.label: Use Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.

questions.explanation: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
questions.hint: Choose the option that acknowledges both benefits and trade-offs.
questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best judgment about when to use Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization?

sections.body: Lazy loading defers code or resources until a feature is needed, reducing initial work while adding loading and error states.
sections.title: Lazy loading

sections.body: Code splitting emits separate bundles loaded on demand, often at route or feature boundaries.
sections.title: Code splitting

sections.body: Bundle size affects download, parse, and execution time. Measure real user conditions and audit large dependencies.
sections.title: Bundle size

sections.body: Begin with clear, correct code, profile a real bottleneck, change one factor, and measure again.
sections.title: Avoiding premature optimization

summary: Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization connects Lazy loading, Code splitting, Bundle size.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Code Splitting and Pragmatic Optimization

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns code splitting and pragmatic optimization from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Performance and Advanced Effects** module.
